scenario: Helios Bank models a Camunda 8.8 DMN routing table that handles 12000 loan applications per month. A modeler wants to declare that the season input always converts to a string, and the team must decide whether to set the typeRef attribute on the input clause and on which exact XML element that attribute belongs in Camunda 8.8 DMN files.
question: Which XML attribute declares the type of a DMN input clause, and on which element does it sit in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Place typeRef on the input element wrapping the clause to declare the data type of the entire input clause in Camunda 8.8 DMN tables
Place typeRef on the decisionTable element so that every input clause inherits the same shared data type during evaluation in Camunda 8.8 DMN
Place typeRef on the inputExpression element to declare the data type of the input clause, as documented for Camunda 8.8 DMN decision tables
Place typeRef on a nested type element under the input clause, which the engine reads before the FEEL expression begins evaluation in Camunda 8.8

a.text: Incorrect. The docs place typeRef on the inputExpression element, not on the parent input element, so the type declaration belongs one level deeper inside the clause.

b.text: Incorrect. The decisionTable element does not carry a typeRef in DMN; each input clause sets its own type on its inputExpression element.

c.text: Correct. The docs specify that typeRef sits on the inputExpression XML element to declare the data type of the input clause in Camunda 8.8.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no separate type element in the DMN schema for this purpose; typeRef is an attribute directly on the inputExpression element.

explanation: Camunda 8.8 DMN declares the input clause type with a typeRef attribute on the inputExpression XML element, which lets the engine validate the converted input value against the chosen data type.
